mnemonics.title: Array Index Rule
mnemonics.content: ALWAYS START AT ZERO - ARRAY INDICES IN C BEGIN FROM 0, NOT 1. REMEMBER: arr[0] IS FIRST, arr[n-1] IS LAST.

mnemonics.title: Structure vs Union
mnemonics.content: STRUCT: ALL DIFFERENT (EACH HAS OWN SPACE) - UNION: UNITED AS ONE (SHARE SAME SPACE)

acronyms.expansion: MEMORY ALLOCATION - Allocates specified bytes from heap, returns void pointer
CONTIGUOUS ALLOCATION - Allocates and initializes to zero
RELEASES Dynamically allocated memory back to heap
acronyms.usage: free(ptr); - Always free after use to prevent memory leak

visualTips.visualization: Think of a 2D array as a ROW-MAJOR rectangle: each row is stored left-to-right, rows are stored top-to-bottom. Address = base + (row × columns + column) × size
Structure is like a FORM/PAPER with multiple FIELDS: each field occupies its own space. Total size is sum of all fields (with padding)
Union is like a SHARED DESK: only one item can be on the desk at a time. All fields point to the same memory location
Dynamic array is like a RESIZABLE CONTAINER: malloc creates initial space, realloc expands/shrinks it while preserving existing data

analogies.analogy: Think of an array as a PARKING LOT with numbered spaces (indices): each space holds one car (element), and you can directly go to space number 5 to find your car in O(1) time.
Structure is like a STUDENT ID CARD containing multiple different fields: name (text), age (number), grade (character) - all different types grouped together.
Array of structures is like a CLASSROOM with rows of desks: each desk (structure) has the same format (name, roll number, marks), but different students sit at each desk.
Static array is like a FIXED-SIZE HOTEL with pre-booked rooms, while dynamic memory is like RENTING APARTMENTS: you can rent more or fewer as your family grows or shrinks.
Array name is like a STREET ADDRESS sign: it points to where the first house (element) begins. Walking down the street (pointer arithmetic) visits each house in order.
